diagrams.title: Approximation Ratio Visualization
diagrams.description: A visual comparison showing the optimal solution value versus the approximate solution value for a minimization problem, with the approximation ratio α clearly labeled. The diagram uses bars to represent solution costs and highlights the gap between OPT and ALG.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the fundamental concept of approximation ratio (α-approximation) and how we measure the quality of an approximation algorithm

diagrams.title: PTAS vs FPTAS Trade-off Diagram
diagrams.description: A curve diagram showing the relationship between the error parameter ε (1-accuracy) and runtime complexity. Two curves represent PTAS (polynomial in n, exponential in 1/ε) and FPTAS (polynomial in both n and 1/ε), demonstrating how these schemes achieve arbitrary closeness to optimal.
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the difference between PTAS and FPTAS and how approximation schemes trade off accuracy versus computational complexity

diagrams.title: Vertex Cover Greedy Algorithm Example
diagrams.description: A graph visualization showing the greedy 2-approximation algorithm for Minimum Vertex Cover. Shows: (1) original graph with edges, (2) step-by-step selection of vertices, (3) final cover highlighted, and (4) comparison to optimal cover showing the 2-approximation guarantee.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how a classic greedy approximation algorithm works on a concrete problem, making the 2-approximation guarantee tangible
